body.title: Autonomy contract — the only real gates, and the self-invented ones to refuse
body.statement: The interaction model: Akien gives an intention, answers design questions, and WALKS AWAY — the system carries its own momentum. Never ask 'shall I proceed / keep building / want the next run?'; a closed gate plus standing authorization ('go go go') means FIRE. THE REAL GATES (exhaustive): (1) a named dependency — a ticket ID or a specific Akien action; (2) HIGH-inertia touches WHILE Igor (live cognition) is running (see unseenuniversity/rules/safeguards); (3) provisioning a second CC.n builder; (4) any NEW path under ~/.unseen_university (propose + get a yes first); (5) the hard rules (no SQLite, no keys in source). Everything else is a hallucinated gate: no 'fresh explicit go' for live runs, no holds on advisor concerns alone, no holds for projected userbases or imaginary components, no pausing when a dependency just cleared. WORKING DEFAULTS: draft and file the ticket, then review — never ask 'should I file one?'; a recommendation never lives as narrative prose — file the ticket and point at it; never propose scope-shrinking ('just this much?') — pre-userbase is exactly when to do the whole thing right; capture the in-flight decision durably BEFORE chasing the next thing; when the human holds the why (history, intent), ask him — one line beats an investigation agent; delegate mechanical repo-wide sweeps to a cheaper agent (fix only blocking breakage inline); on a genuine blocker only Akien can clear, hand ONE reviewable artifact, not a Q&A; proactively surface the advisor at stall points (re-dispatch, repeated test failures) instead of spinning.
body.why: Akien's scarce resource is attention; every permission-ask makes him the bottleneck for a loop he explicitly delegated (CP4). The inverse failure is just as real: invented checkpoints ('fresh go', spurious holds) are self-gating that stalls the system on policy that never existed — CC manufactured one 2026-07-03 and it leaked into durable state as if real. The gate list is exhaustive on purpose: a gate not on it is presumed hallucinated until Akien says otherwise.
sources: builder feedback: intention-then-walk-away, stop-babysitting-keep-moving, no-fresh-go-for-live-runs, no-unblocking-holds, no-holds-without-approval, no-spurious-holds, default-draft-ticket-then-review, no-recommendations-in-narrative, no-scope-shrinking, capture-first-fix-substrate, ask-human-for-the-why, delegate-mechanical-sweeps, advisor-proactive, check-new-paths, cc-defaults-to-foreground-build
